pr.title: recover: reintroduce prior-campaign decomp via DTK pipeline (110 -> 3,306 matched functions, 38.43%)

summary: Recovers roughly 3 months of prior decompilation work (previously hand-linked/archived) into the reset dtk-template repo by re-registering every recovered function as a non-linked Object(CodeCandidate) so objdiff can score them without touching the retail main.dol sha1 gate. Raised matched functions from 110 to 3,306 (1.28% -> 38.43%) and matched code from 0.14% to 9.98%.
decomp_lessons: Recovered/archived decomp can be re-integrated safely as CodeCandidate (compared but not linked) so it scores in objdiff/decomp.dev without risking the build-gating main.dol sha1 match.

Per-object mw_version overrides matter: the reset repo defaulted the wrong MWCC version per library; game/*, trk, hsd, crt/stdio need GC/1.3, crt/mem needs GC/2.0, crt/exit needs GC/1.3.2, and dolphin/* needs GC/1.2.5n. Using the archive's compile_config.json to restore these was required for byte-exact matches.

asm{ #include "*.inc" } wrapper functions can falsely register as 100% matches (asm-vs-asm comparison) because objdiff compares generated asm, not real C; a clean checkout without the gitignored .inc files won't build, so a CI build against a clean checkout is the real integrity gate, not the reported match percentage.

When mass-recovering archived work, regenerate object_map.freeze.json and verify total_functions stays invariant (topology-delta-verified) so the split refactor doesn't silently drop or duplicate units.

Percent-functions-matched and percent-code-matched diverge a lot when the recovered set skews toward small/leaf functions: 38% of functions was only ~10% of code bytes, so large/hard functions remain the real remaining work.

Genuinely hard files (heavy asm, draft-only) are better left deferred/excluded from a recovery PR than force-included: sound_se, sound.c core, people.c, colosseum_script.c, menu_bag.c were explicitly deferred here.
smart_moves: Verified every counted function was byte-exact (fuzzy_match_percent == 100) and build-gated rather than trusting the archived campaign's own bookkeeping.
Ran an independent recount against report.json (3,306 functions at fuzzy 100%) instead of trusting the first pass, which is what caught 35 falsely-counted asm-wrapper functions in gs_field_world.c.

After finding the asm-wrapper false positives, shipped a follow-up commit (cb421e03) that removed 63 undecompiled asm functions down to forward declarations and fixed a Shift-JIS encoding bug, then re-verified by building with all .inc files removed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: New split units carve genuinely-decompiled translation units out of larger auto-generated units, keyed by function address ranges in splits.txt.
check_object_map_freeze.py validates object_map.freeze.json topology (unit/function counts) hasn't silently drifted across a large split refactor.

key_files.role: Adds ~450 lines registering recovered TUs as Object(CodeCandidate, ...) with correct per-library mw_version overrides
Regenerated frozen object/function map (4164 lines added), verified topology-invariant at 318 units / 8603 functions
312 new split entries carving genuinely-decompiled TUs out of auto-generated units
New verification script (280 lines) asserting object_map.freeze.json topology invariants hold after the recovery
15024 lines added; source of the asm-wrapper false-positive bug fixed in follow-up commit cb421e03
308-line handoff doc explaining the recovery approach and verification steps for future agents
